reasoning: The Overnight Bank Funding Rate is at 3.89 as of Dec 5, 2025 (the value for Dec 7). Markets currently place an 87% probability on a 25 basis point rate cut at the upcoming Dec 9-10 FOMC meeting, which would drop the OBFR to approximately 3.64. The Fed is in an easing cycle, meaning future meetings (including late January and mid-March 2026) are highly likely to result in further cuts or holds, rather than hikes. Even if the Fed holds rates in December (a 13% chance), the OBFR would likely fluctuate between 3.88 and 3.90, making an increase above the 3.89 baseline unlikely. Thus, the probability of the rate being higher on Dec 14, Jan 6, and Mar 7 is very small, capped essentially by the outside chance of a Fed hold combined with a minor upward drift, or an unexpected inflation spike leading to future rate hikes. I incremented probabilities slightly for further time horizons to reflect growing uncertainty.
